creator: mheffner@vt.edu
text:
On 10-Sep-2001 john_m_cooper@yahoo.com wrote:
| The following reply was made to PR bin/30490; it has been noted by GNATS.
| 
| From: <john_m_cooper@yahoo.com>
| To: <freebsd-gnats-submit@FreeBSD.org>, <john_m_cooper@yahoo.com>
| Cc: <sobomax@FreeBSD.org>, <glewis@eyesbeyond.com>
| Subject: Re: bin/30490: yacc in world does NOT fully respect TMPDIR setting
| Date: Mon, 10 Sep 2001 11:48:45 -0700
| 
|  I believe I have isolated the code in /usr/src/usr.bin/yacc that produces
|  the error to main.c.
|  

What's the exact error message? I've looked at yacc and it doesn't appear to
have a problem with using TMPDIR, however I could be wrong. I've also tested
yacc with a different temp directory and it didn't give me any problems. Perhaps
it's a different build tool that has a problem with TMPDIR?


Mike

-- 
  Mike Heffner     <mheffner@[acm.]vt.edu>
  Blacksburg, VA       <mikeh@FreeBSD.org>
